plainEnglish: Moving keyboard focus to an element must not trigger an unexpected change of context—like submitting a form, opening a new window, or navigating away. Focusing a field should never surprise the user; wait for an explicit action (a click or Enter) to make big changes.
whyItMatters: Keyboard and screen reader users get disoriented when simply tabbing onto a control unexpectedly navigates away or opens a new window.
quickCheck: Tab through every control and confirm that merely focusing it never submits a form, opens a window, or navigates — nothing should change context on focus alone.
commonMistakes: Using onFocus to auto-submit a form or navigate to a new page.
Opening a popup or new window when a field receives focus.
Auto-advancing to the next field on focus rather than after input is complete.

codeExamples.label: Focus alone changes nothing
codeExamples.code:
{/* Focus just focuses — navigation happens only on explicit submit */}
<select onChange={handleChange}>{/* options */}</select>

codeExamples.label: Focusing the field navigates away
codeExamples.code:
{/* Tabbing onto the field unexpectedly changes context */}
<select onFocus={() => (window.location.href = "/next")}>{/* options */}</select>
